findings.description: Repeated page header/footer content in HTML
findings.evidence: The following text blocks repeat 3+ times, likely page headers/footers that should not appear in the body: 'where,' (3x).

findings.description: Duplicated company/license text on cover page. 'Geosyntec Consultants of NC, P.C.' and 'NC License No.: C-3500 and C-295' appear twice in the HTML — once extracted from the logo image area and once as standalone text.
findings.evidence: PDF shows this text once below the Geosyntec logo. HTML renders it twice in succession before the title block.

findings.description: Figure 1 (Outfall 002 Sampling Locations aerial map) is rendered as a tiny, barely legible thumbnail in the HTML, with its text labels decomposed into separate text elements. The full aerial/satellite map showing facility layout, sampling locations, inset detail, and spatial context is effectively lost.
findings.evidence: PDF shows a full-page aerial map with labeled sampling locations, inset detail of Outfall 002, scale bars, and legend. HTML shows only a small cropped thumbnail of the 'River Water Intake at Facility' area, with labels like '24-hr Composite and Continuous Flow', '3.5-day Composite', scale bars, and legend items rendered as plain text outside the image.

findings.description: Figure 3 flowchart content appears both as a flowchart image and as a text rendition of the same decision logic, creating duplicated reading of the same content.
findings.evidence: PDF shows Figure 3 as a single flowchart image. HTML shows the flowchart image AND a separate text rendition below it: 'Was a 24-hr composite sample collected? YES: Use 24-hr composite sample concentration... NO: Use 60-day rolling average dry concentration.'

findings.description: Repeated header/footer branding text inserted inline between body content sections, disrupting reading flow.
findings.evidence: PDF page headers ('Geosyntec consultants', 'Geosyntec Consultants of NC, P.C.', 'NC License No.: C-3500 and C-295') appear inline between Section 3.6 and the TABLES section in the HTML, interrupting the document flow.

findings.description:
Figure 3 (Flowchart for Determining the Concentration for Loading at the River Water Intake) from PDF page 17 is missing as an image. The HTML renders its content only as a textual list (
    ) within a
findings.evidence: PDF page 17 contains a visual flowchart diagram with decision diamonds and outcome boxes. HTML has: — no tag is present, unlike Figure 2 which does have an tag.

findings.description:
Figure 1 map annotation labels that are part of the map image in the PDF are also extracted as separate

elements in the HTML body, creating duplication of content that exists within the image.
findings.evidence:
HTML contains separate paragraphs: '

River Water Intake at Facility

', '

24-hr Composite and Continuous Flow

', '

3.5-day Composite

', '

0 25 50 ft

', '

0 500 1000 ft

', '

Legend
Sampling Location

' — these are text labels that appear ON the Figure 1 map image in the PDF (page 15) and should not be rendered as separate body text alongside the image.

findings.description: Geosyntec Consultants logo from PDF page headers appears three times in the HTML body. The logo repeats on every page of the PDF as a page header but should be consolidated rather than appearing mid-content.
findings.evidence: HTML contains three tags: (1) at the title page, (2) before the TABLE OF CONTENTS heading, and (3) after Section 3.6 before the TABLES heading. Occurrences #2 and #3 are PDF page headers inserted into the body flow.

findings.description: The Geosyntec license text from the Figure 3 title block appears at the very end of the HTML body as a stray paragraph, appearing as repeated header/footer content in the body flow.
findings.evidence:
HTML ends with: '

Geosyntec Consultants of NC, P.C.
NC License No.: C 3500 and C 295

' — this is from the title block of Figure 3 on PDF page 17, appearing as body content after all figure content has ended.
